elements.subsection-2411-003: Positive incremental moduli for compressible response
elements.subsection-2411-004: Positive incremental moduli for incompressible response and Hill's inequality
elements.sent-2411-073: In the incompressible case ($\det F = 1$), the Cauchy stress tensor $\sigma$ can be identified with the Kirchhoff stress tensor $\tau= \det F \cdot \sigma$.
elements.sent-2411-074: Moreover, incompressibility allows to nicely by-pass the stress conditions at the free surface and the constitutive law.
elements.sent-2411-075: Instead one can immediately arrive at the kinematics for the family $t \mapsto F(t)$.
elements.sent-2411-076: Only in a second step do we need to calculate the indetermined pressure $p$ from the boundary conditions in order to finally obtain the explicit form of the principal stresses.
elements.sent-2411-077: We will see that Hill's inequality (applied to the incompressible response) is equivalent to (CSP) and already implies positive incremental moduli.
elements.sent-2411-078: Let us explain with three examples the content of the above development.
elements.sent-2411-079: We consider the uniaxial tension of a cube of homogeneous and isotropic elastic material.
elements.sent-2411-080: Upon loading the cube deforms homogeneously into a parallelepiped and $F(t)$ remains diagonal, thus rotation effects are absent in the corotational derivative and the principal axes remain constant.
